label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why a lot of figures are used in the text. The options are A through D. First, I need to understand the purpose of the figures in the text. The passage mentions several percentages and growth rates. For example, about 30% of urban households have a credit card, a 40% average growth rate from 2004 to 2009, 90% of non-users unaware of safety issues, 60% have little knowledge of debt consequences, 72% never late on payments, 55% pay full balance, 70% say credit cards led to overspending. These figures are presented to support the points made by Rui Yao and the study's findings. The question is about the reason for using so many figures. Let's look at the options: A) record the valuable data – This seems too vague. It's not just about recording data, but using it for a purpose. B) provide concrete evidence – This makes sense because the figures back up the claims made in the study. The author is using statistical data to support the arguments about the implications of credit card usage in China. C) introduce a research method – The text doesn't describe the methodology of the research. It just states the findings, so this is probably not correct.
